content: read reviews/88.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/88.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/88.md.

    49→- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 43–50).
    50→- [x] Docstring references `[KSSS22]`.
    51→
    52→### `answer()` usage
    53→
    54→The original problem is a direct assertion ("if G has no clique or independent set of size ≥ ε log n, then G contains an induced subgraph with exactly m edges for every m ≤ δn²"). This is **Case 3** — problem stated as a direct assertion.
    55→
    56→- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
    57→- [x] No misplaced quantifiers (all quantifiers are in the theorem body, not as parameters).

    68→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.

   108→- The formalization captures the Erdős–McKay conjecture faithfully: for any ε > 0 there exists δ > 0 such that graphs on n vertices with no clique or independent set of size ≥ ⌈ε · log n⌉ contain induced subgraphs realizing every edge count up to δn². The use of `Gᶜ.CliqueFree` for the independent set condition is correct (independent sets in G = cliques in Gᶜ).
   109→- Category `research solved` is appropriate (proved by Kwan–Sah–Sauermann–Sawhney, 2023; not formally verified).

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/88.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–31).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 88`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link:
  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/88](https://www.erdosproblems.com/88)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Erdős–McKay conjecture on induced subgraphs with prescribed edge counts).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[KSSS22]` — Kwan, Sah, Sauermann, Sawhney (2023).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace: `namespace Erdos88` … `end Erdos88` (lines 35, 63).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos88`.
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block: `open SimpleGraph Finset Real` (line 33).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `inducedEdgeCount` (line 39) has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 37–38).
- [x] Definition is computable (uses `DecidableRel`, `Finset.filter`, `.card`); `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Function returning `ℕ` uses `lowerCamelCase`: `inducedEdgeCount`.
- [x] No `local notation` needed (none used; N/A).
- [ ] *Optional:* No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity-check provided for `inducedEdgeCount`. Consider adding one to verify correctness on a small example.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problem uses the `theorem` keyword (line 52).
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_88` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` (line 51).
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 43–50).
- [x] Docstring references `[KSSS22]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem is a direct assertion ("if G has no clique or independent set of size ≥ ε log n, then G contains an induced subgraph with exactly m edges for every m ≤ δn²"). This is **Case 3** — problem stated as a direct assertion.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly, with no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] No misplaced quantifiers (all quantifiers are in the theorem body, not as parameters).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (conjecture proved by Kwan–Sah–Sauermann–Sawhney but not formally verified).
- [x] Not `research open` with `sorry` — correctly marked `research solved`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5` (combinatorics).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single entry; trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` + `sorry` on separate lines (`:= by\n  sorry`, lines 60–61). Acceptable multi-line format.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. Only the main theorem `erdos_88`.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (each nesting level adds 2 spaces consistently).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `ε`, `δ`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `→`, `∧`, `≤`, `ᶜ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] All lines under 100 characters.
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All substantive checklist items pass:

- Copyright, import, docstring, and namespace are all correctly formatted.
- `inducedEdgeCount` is a reasonable helper definition with a proper docstring, correct casing, and no `sorry`.
- The formalization captures the Erdős–McKay conjecture faithfully: for any ε > 0 there exists δ > 0 such that graphs on n vertices with no clique or independent set of size ≥ ⌈ε · log n⌉ contain induced subgraphs realizing every edge count up to δn². The use of `Gᶜ.CliqueFree` for the independent set condition is correct (independent sets in G = cliques in Gᶜ).
- Category `research solved` is appropriate (proved by Kwan–Sah–Sauermann–Sawhney, 2023; not formally verified).
- AMS code `5` (combinatorics) is correct.
- No code quality issues.

**Minor note:** Consider adding a `@[category test]` example to verify `inducedEdgeCount` on a small graph (optional, not required).

content.text: The review status is **PASS**. No changes needed.
